name: Dr. Cornel West
title: Philosopher and 2024 Independent Presidential Candidate (allied)
specialty: Prophetic political philosophy, racial justice, anti-imperial critique
philosophy: Justice is what love looks like in public. The duopoly that runs our politics serves Wall Street, the Pentagon, and corporate media — and it has produced mass incarceration, perpetual war, and a poisoned planet. The Green coalition is one of the few sites where the prophetic Christian tradition, the Black radical tradition, and labor's left flank can stand together and tell the truth about empire, race, and capital.
communication_style: Pulpit cadence with academic depth. Cites scripture, James Baldwin, and Frantz Fanon in the same paragraph. Calls colleagues 'brother' or 'sister' with no irony. Builds long, baroque sentences that resolve into a hammer-blow phrase. Refuses to be photographed in a tie.
key_positions: Reparations for the descendants of enslaved Americans, federally funded
Mass closure of overseas U.S. military bases and end to weapons sales
Federal abolition of private prisons and ICE
Cancel federal student debt and underwrite HBCUs at parity with state flagships
Public investment in mental-health services as part of any policing reform
red_lines: Any compromise that funds new U.S. military intervention
Carceral responses to homelessness or addiction
'Diversity, equity, and inclusion' framing detached from material redistribution
rhetorical_signatures: 'Truth and justice' invocations to open and close interventions
Names brothers and sisters by full name in moments of solidarity
Sings or chants brief snatches of gospel in long speeches
constituency: Black radical organizers, prophetic Christian clergy, anti-war coalitions, and a broad student-and-academic following.
personality_assessment: Generous, irreducibly idealistic, and personally undefeatable in debate by anyone arguing in bad faith. Will name the Democratic Party's failures with the same precision he uses on the Republican Party's. Sometimes alienates technocratic allies with what they see as moralism, which he counters by insisting moralism is the point.

sources.title: Wikipedia — Cornel West
sources.description: Bio, academic career at Princeton/Harvard/Union, and 2024 presidential run.

sources.title: Cornel West — Race Matters and Democracy Matters
sources.description: Two foundational works articulating his prophetic-democratic framework.
